title: The murder of Karina Holmer remains unsolved 30 years later; police asking public for help
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Boston Police continue to investigate the 1996 murder of Swedish au pair Karina Holmer, who was last seen at a nightclub.
Holmer's body was found dismembered in a dumpster; the crime scene has never been located and her lower half remains missing.
Police urge the public to share any information, hoping new leads will surface 30 years after Holmer's unsolved murder.

title: Brad Stevens ‘lost a lot of sleep’ over the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Brad Stevens expressed regret and sleepless nights over the decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Celtics traded Brown due to salary cap constraints, acquiring Paul George and four draft picks in exchange.
Stevens acknowledged Brown's dissatisfaction with the trade process, emphasizing open discussions throughout negotiations.

title: From ‘under the weather’ to ‘scuttlebutt’: The sailing phrases you’ll hear at Sail Boston 250
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 will feature over 60 Tall Ships in Boston Harbor from July 11-16, offering public tours.
Attendees can learn nautical phrases like 'under the weather' and 'scuttlebutt' linked to maritime history.
Visitors can interact with crew members and experience traditional maritime practices during the event.

title: Bill Belichick was reportedly considered for a temporary head coach role with the Broncos
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Bill Belichick was considered for a temporary head coach role with the Denver Broncos by Sean Payton.
Sean Payton planned for Belichick to lead the Broncos until breaking Don Shula's win record, then return as assistant.
The plan was deemed too complicated and was not implemented; Belichick later coached at University of North Carolina.

title: An update from a 2019 letter writer
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: A 2019 letter writer shares how a breakup discussion led to a stronger relationship through advice and self-reflection.
The couple engaged in therapy after reading the advice column, resulting in a deeper understanding and overcoming past pressures.
The couple is now happily married, having embraced growth and adventures together before ultimately tying the knot.

title: Officials: Man who shot 2 in Hampton Beach before dying by suicide was active-duty Navy sailor
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tyshawn Cooper, 28, an active-duty Navy sailor, shot two people in Hampton, New Hampshire before dying by suicide.
The shooting victims, a 23-year-old man and a 25-year-old woman, remain hospitalized with their injuries.
Cooper was assigned to USS Hampton, a submarine undergoing maintenance in Kittery, Maine, officials confirmed.

title: Report: Celtics’ Paul George turned down $3.9 million trade bonus
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul George waived his $3.9 million trade bonus, keeping his cap hit at $54.1 million with the Celtics.
The move provides salary cap relief for the Celtics, who are $30 million over the cap and navigating luxury tax penalties.
Boston aims to stay below the severe second apron penalties, having traded players like Jrue Holiday and Kristaps Porzingis.

title: R.I. food bank thanks Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce for pre-wedding donation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated $1 million to the Rhode Island Community Food Bank before their New York wedding.
The donation will help purchase and distribute nutritious food to local families across Rhode Island's 137 member agencies.
Other charities, including Feeding America and Harvesters, also received significant donations from the couple before their marriage.

title: 5 takeaways from the Celtics’ press conference about the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and draft assets to balance team finances.
Brad Stevens emphasized the trade was based on financial strategy, not mandates, with future roster flexibility in mind.
Celtics' reputation questioned following trade due to perceived mistreatment of Brown during his 10-year tenure.

title: 5 takeaways from the U.S. loss to Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The U.S. was defeated 4-1 by Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16 in Seattle.
Belgium's lineup changes included omitting Kevin De Bruyne and adding Nicolas Raskin and Dodi Lukébakio.
The U.S. lacked defensive intensity and attacking coherence, with crucial errors from goalkeeper Matt Freese.
